instruction: Write a professional email in the first person to ask your boss for a raise. The email must include at least three reasons justifying the request, such as your recent achievements, increased responsibilities, or market comparisons. Ensure the email is written in a formal tone and contains exactly five sentences. The phrase "value to the company" must appear at least twice, and the keywords "raise" and "performance" must appear in the first and last sentences, respectively. All sentences should be written in lowercase. Incorporate humor and colloquial expressions into the email.
response: i am writing to formally request a raise because my recent performance has consistently delivered immense value to the company while handling a workload that would make a sloth look like a sprinter. over the past year, i have taken on new responsibilities, secured key clients, and proven my value to the company by turning chaos into organized chaos-free zones. considering that my contributions have outpaced the average employee by a margin that would make a calculator weep, it seems fair to align my compensation with current market rates. i believe that adjusting my salary now will not only reward my hard work but also ensure i can continue bringing that same value to the company for years to come. i hope you will consider this request seriously, as my continued dedication and performance are ready to take us both to the next level.

Conflicts between Style Constraints.new_constraint: Incorporate humor and colloquial expressions into the email.
Conflicts between Style Constraints.org_constraint: Ensure the email is written in a formal tone.
